name: DemoFoundry — Self demo (Playwright)
description: A fully click-driven tour of DemoFoundry's own web UI. Every step has a real Playwright selector against the app on localhost:3000, so the capture path can drive the whole New-demo flow hands-free. Run with the web capture mode (demofoundry render --url http://localhost:3000 --steps this-file).

steps.narration_text: This is DemoFoundry — and this demo was made by DemoFoundry, driving its own interface. We start in the Library, where every project lives.
To begin a new demo, you click New demo.
First you pick how to capture. A web app gets driven automatically in a browser — that's this path. A desktop app you record yourself. We're demoing a web app.
You name the demo. This is just the label you'll see back in your Library.
Then you point it at the running app you want to demo — usually your own app on localhost. Stable test IDs make capture most reliable.
Now the script — an ordered list of actions and the words to narrate. We'll load the built-in sample to see the shape of one.
The script is just JSON. You can paste your own, drop a steps.json file onto the upload zone, or edit it right here.

steps.value: A quick tour of the invoicing flow — open a new invoice, add a customer and line items, apply a discount, and send it.
steps.narration_text: You can also describe what you're demoing in plain English, and let Claude write any narration your steps don't already include.
All the while, the What we read panel parses your script live — every scene, the action it'll take, and where it'll zoom or highlight.

From here you'd pick a voice and render. DemoFoundry drives your app once, narrates each scene, syncs the video to the audio, and composes a captioned MP4. Point it at your app, give it a script, and ship a polished demo.
